title: Chapter 5 - Summary Measures
description: A hard-difficulty multiple-choice quiz on measures of central tendency, dispersion, and other summary measures.
subject: DSC1371 - Business Statistics

questions.question: For a dataset with an even number of observations, what is the correct method to calculate the median?
options: The value of the middle observation.
The average of the two middle observations.
The most frequently occurring value.
The difference between the maximum and minimum observations.
questions.answer: The average of the two middle observations.
questions.rationale: The median for an even number of observations is the average of the two central values when the data is arranged in order of magnitude. This differs from the median of an odd number of observations, which is the single central value.

questions.question: Which of the following measures of central tendency is most affected by extreme values (outliers)?
options: Mean
Median
Mode
Interquartile Range
questions.rationale: The mean is calculated using all observations, so a single extreme value can significantly alter its value. The median and mode, which are based on position and frequency respectively, are not affected by outliers in the same way.

questions.question: A dataset has a mean of 75 and a median of 85. Based on this information, what can be inferred about the shape of the data's distribution?
options: It is symmetric.
It is positively skewed.
It is negatively skewed.
It is bimodal.
questions.rationale: When a distribution is negatively skewed (skewed to the left), the tail of the distribution is longer on the left side, causing the mean to be pulled down by low extreme values. This results in the mean being less than the median.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using the median over the mean for a skewed distribution?
options: The median is easier to calculate.
The median provides a better measure of the true center of the data.
The median is always a whole number.
The mean is not affected by skewness.
questions.answer: The median provides a better measure of the true center of the data.
questions.rationale: The median is resistant to the influence of extreme values, unlike the mean. For a skewed distribution, the median more accurately represents the typical or central value, as it is not pulled by the skew.

questions.question: In a dataset with values {10, 15, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40}, what is the interquartile range?
questions.rationale: The first quartile (QL) is the median of the lower half of the data, and the third quartile (QU) is the median of the upper half. For the given dataset, QL is 15 and QU is 30. The interquartile range is QU - QL = 30 - 15 = 15.

questions.question: Which of the following measures is most suitable for describing a categorical variable?
options: Mean
Median
Mode
Standard deviation
questions.rationale: The mode is the only measure of central tendency that can be used for categorical data, as it simply represents the most frequent category or value.

questions.question: What does a larger standard deviation indicate about a dataset compared to a smaller one?
options: The data is more clustered around the mean.
The data has a lower mean.
The data is more spread out from the mean.
The data is bimodal.
questions.answer: The data is more spread out from the mean.
questions.rationale: Standard deviation measures the average distance of each observation from the mean. A larger value indicates that the data points are, on average, farther from the mean, signifying greater dispersion or variability.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of the mean as a measure of central tendency?
options: It is easy to calculate by hand.
It is not affected by outliers.
It is always a value present in the dataset.
It uses all the data in its calculation.
questions.answer: It uses all the data in its calculation.
questions.rationale: The mean's value is derived from the sum of all observations, making it a comprehensive measure that reflects the entire dataset. This is also its weakness, as it makes it sensitive to outliers.

questions.question: If you have a dataset with a median of 45, what percentage of the data points are at or below this value?
questions.rationale: The median is the value that separates the upper half of a data set from the lower half. Therefore, 50% of the observations are at or below the median.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of dispersion?
options: Mode
Median
Quartiles
Range
questions.rationale: The Range is a measure of dispersion, which quantifies the spread of data. The others are measures of central tendency or position.

questions.question: How is the Coefficient of Variation (CV) primarily used?
options: To determine the skewness of a distribution.
To find the mean of a dataset.
To compare the variability of two or more distributions with different units or scales.
To calculate the quartiles of a dataset.
questions.answer: To compare the variability of two or more distributions with different units or scales.
questions.rationale: The Coefficient of Variation is a unitless measure of relative variability. It is the ratio of the standard deviation to the mean, making it useful for comparing the spread of datasets with different units or vastly different means.

questions.question: The 'five-number summary' of a distribution, which is used to construct a Box Plot, includes which of the following measures?
options: Mean, Median, Mode, Range, and Standard Deviation
Minimum, Maximum, Quartile 1, Median, and Quartile 3
Mean, Quartile 1, Quartile 3, Interquartile Range, and Standard Deviation
Mean, Minimum, Maximum, Interquartile Range, and Range
questions.answer: Minimum, Maximum, Quartile 1, Median, and Quartile 3
questions.rationale: The five-number summary consists of the minimum observation, the lower quartile (Q1), the median, the upper quartile (Q3), and the maximum observation.

questions.question: What does the 'box' in a Box Plot represent?
options: The full range of the data.
The middle 50% of the data, from the lower to the upper quartile.
The mean and standard deviation.
The median and mode.
questions.answer: The middle 50% of the data, from the lower to the upper quartile.
questions.rationale: The box in a box plot is drawn from the lower quartile (QL) to the upper quartile (QU), representing the distribution of the middle 50% of the data, also known as the interquartile range.

questions.question: A data point is considered a 'probable outlier' in a Box Plot if it falls outside of which calculated limits?
options: The box itself.
The inner fences.
The outer fences.
The whiskers.
questions.rationale: The document defines 'probable outliers' as points that are more extreme than the outer fences. The outer fences are calculated as QL - 3(QU - QL) and QU + 3(QU - QL).

questions.question: Which of the following describes the relationship between variance and standard deviation?
options: Standard deviation is the square of the variance.
Variance is the square root of the standard deviation.
Standard deviation is the square root of the variance.
They are two different names for the same measure.
questions.answer: Standard deviation is the square root of the variance.
questions.rationale: The standard deviation is the square root of the variance. This relationship is used to bring the measure of dispersion back to the same unit as the original data.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of a 'Summary Measure'?
options: To present raw data in an unorganized way.
To find the maximum and minimum values of a dataset.
To reduce large amounts of data into a simple, sensible summary.
To create a visual representation of a frequency distribution.
questions.answer: To reduce large amounts of data into a simple, sensible summary.
questions.rationale: The document states that descriptive statistics, which include summary measures, help us 'simply large amounts of data in a sensible way' and that 'each descriptive statistic reduces lots of data into a simpler summary'.

questions.question: The Interquartile Range (IQR) is considered a robust measure of dispersion because:
options: It is based on all data points.
It is highly sensitive to extreme values.
It is not affected by outliers.
It is always a whole number.
questions.answer: It is not affected by outliers.
questions.rationale: The IQR is the difference between the third and first quartiles. Since it measures the spread of the middle 50% of the data, it is not influenced by extreme values at the ends of the distribution, making it a robust measure.

questions.question: When comparing the variability of two investment portfolios, one with an average return of 10% and the other with an average return of 5%, which measure is most appropriate?
options: Range
Standard Deviation
Coefficient of Variation
Interquartile Range
questions.rationale: The Coefficient of Variation is ideal for this scenario because it allows for a comparison of variability between two datasets that have different means and scales. It normalizes the standard deviation by the mean, providing a relative measure of risk.

questions.question: A dataset has a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 5. Another dataset has a mean of 100 and a standard deviation of 10. Which dataset is more dispersed relative to its mean?
options: The first dataset (mean 50, standard deviation 5).
The second dataset (mean 100, standard deviation 10).
They have the same relative dispersion.
Cannot be determined without a Box Plot.
questions.answer: They have the same relative dispersion.
questions.rationale: The Coefficient of Variation (CV) is used to compare relative dispersion. CV = (Standard Deviation / Mean) * 100%. For the first dataset, CV = (5/50) * 100% = 10%. For the second dataset, CV = (10/100) * 100% = 10%. Since their CVs are equal, their relative dispersion is the same.

questions.question: In a Box Plot, a data point is considered a 'possible outlier' if it lies between:
options: The minimum and the lower quartile.
The lower quartile and the median.
The inner and outer fences.
The upper quartile and the maximum.
questions.rationale: The document specifies that 'points lying between the inner and outer fences are known as possible outliers'. The inner fences are located at QL - 1.5(QU - QL) and QU + 1.5(QU - QL). The outer fences are located at QL - 3(QU - QL) and QU + 3(QU - QL).
